title: plantago humilis subsp. granatensis
intro: Plantago humilis subsp. granatensis, a subspecies of the common plantain, is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the treatment of digestive issues. The health benefits of Plantago humilis subsp. granatensis include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be taken to alleviate various health problems.
